examples: Ridges of peat and pools of dark, acidic water form a patterned peatland near Moon Point.
Peatland can store over 5,000 tonnes of carbon per hectare, and when drained for cultivation emits it for decades.

Survey work is taking place to calculate how much carbon dioxide is stored within peatland in Cumbria.
Located in the far north of the Scottish Highlands, the area encompasses around 2000 sq km of mountain, peatland, beach, forest and coastline across west Sutherland and on to the north coast.
